title: invitations — Laravel notifications
description: Four notifications this module owns. All classified `transactional` (essential) — invitations are directly-solicited communications, so opt-out does not apply for the invitation itself. Downstream consent captured on accept (the invitee explicitly consents to receive further comms).

targets: Invitation (via routeNotificationForMail on the invitation itself using its email column)
notifications.description: The invitation itself. Body: greeting, inviter name, target label, custom message, big CTA button linking to `https://{application.central_host}/invitations/{raw_token}`, expiry note, decline link, unsubscribe-if-not-you link. Localised to the invitee's preferred locale when known; falls back to the tenant's default locale otherwise. Channel selection driven by the invitation's `channel` field.

Sent 48h before an invitation expires if it's still in a non-terminal state. Body reminds the invitee, restates the CTA. Frequency: at most one reminder per invitation (checked via `invitation.metadata.reminder_sent_at`).
Sent to the inviter (a User, not a ServiceAccount — bots don't need email) when the invitee accepts. Body: 'X accepted your invitation to Y', with a link to view the new team member's profile.

targets: User (invitations admins in the tenant, matched by `invitations.manage_any` permission)
notifications.description: Sent to tenant admins when a hard bounce or complaint is recorded. Body includes the invitee email, bounce_reason, and a CTA to correct + resend. Slack channel available for tenants with a wired-in webhook.

consentTierNotes.transactional: Directly-solicited or safety-of-service communications. Not gated by marketing opt-out. Invitations are always transactional at the send level; downstream product communications from Stackra require explicit consent captured on accept.
